title: Cheap Self-Hosted Kubernetes on Hetzner Cloud
excerpt: This blog’s starter project can be found at github.com/qstarsit/hcloud-kube-hetzner Kubernetes is a brilliant workload API, the community has built a highly extensible standard which abstracts the underlying machines and infrastructure from our application deployments. However, the cloud-native ecosystem can come with a sense of vendor lock-in. Functionality like managed load balancing, seamless node autoscaling, extensive storage options, integrated networking, and polished operational tooling are what you pay a premium for.
content_html:
This blog’s starter project can be found at github.com/qstarsit/hcloud-kube-hetzner

Kubernetes is a brilliant workload API, the community has built a highly extensible standard which abstracts the underlying machines and infrastructure from our application deployments. However, the cloud-native ecosystem can come with a sense of vendor lock-in. Functionality like managed load balancing, seamless node autoscaling, extensive storage options, integrated networking, and polished operational tooling are what you pay a premium for.

Running and operating Kubernetes yourself, “The Hard Way”, can be overwhelming, and time intensive. That’s why in this post, we’re taking a look at terraform-hcloud-kube-hetzner. An open-source Terraform project that simplifies deploying a production-ready K3s cluster on Hetzner Cloud, striking a balance between ease of use, reliability, and cost efficiency. All while keeping true to our European origin 🇪🇺.

Hetzner

Hetzner is a German infrastructure provider that has built a strong reputation for offering high-performance hardware at reasonable and predictable prices. With less features than hyperscalers they focus on doing few things very well. This has made them a popular hosting provider among indie hackers and startups alike.

Their Cloud offering has matured a lot in recent years and features a solid API to manage resources. It features private networking, load balancers, network attached storage and virtual machines. This makes it a solid alternative for teams looking for proper automation on a more cost-effective platform.

Hetzner operates modern data centers primarily in Europe, while also offering locations in the USA and Asia. In light of the recent focus on sovereign data centers, the fact that Hetzner is a German company adds reassurance around data sovereignty.

Kube-Hetzner

Kube-Hetzner is an optimized, easy-to-use terraform module to set up a (highly available) auto-upgraded cluster. It codifies many best practices required to run K3s reliably on Hetzner Cloud.

The project handles much of the heavy lifting involved in bootstrapping and maintaining a cluster: provisioning compute resources, configuring private networking, setting up load balancers, managing node pools, and wiring it all together. Upgrades are automated and rolling by default, reducing manual intervention and downtime during maintenance.

It integrates Hetzner’s native tooling directly into your cluster, including the hcloud CSI driver and the hcloud Cloud Controller Manager (CCM). This enables Kubernetes to automatically provision and manage resources like load balancers and block storage via Kubernetes.

MicroOS

In the world of containers, less is more. The same applies for the operating system that functions as the base for our workloads. Traditional server distributions like Ubuntu, Debian or RedHat Enterprise Linux include a wide range of packages and services for general-purpose use, many of which are unnecessary for container workloads. This has led to the development of container-centric operating systems; Talos, CoreOS, Bottlerocket and flatcar just to name a few.

Kube-Hetzner utilises openSUSE’s MicroOS for all of its nodes. MicroOS is a stable operating system with a small footprint specialized in running container workloads. It sports rolling releases, atomic & transactional updates and secure updates by default. The OS is designed to be predictable, resilient and to behave the same on every boot.

MicroOS is immutable and cannot be modified during runtime. Updates are applied transactionally using Btrfs snapshots, allowing the system to fully succeed or roll back to a clean state. In the event of a faulty update a previous snapshot is automatically booted with no manual interaction required.

A read-only root filesystem also reduces our attack surface. After a container is compromised, modifying host binaries or operating system configuration is not possible. Even if an attacker gains host-level access, any changes are discarded on reboot.

Our application workloads are managed independently from the host operating system and are not installed on the root filesystem. This prevents configuration drift and ensures all of our nodes remain interchangeable and can soundly be rolled out, replaced or recovered repeatedly.

K3s

The heart of our cluster consists of K3s, Rancher’s lightweight Kubernetes distribution. Designed specifically for compute-constrained environments like edge or IoT devices. K3s packages components like the Kubernetes API server, kube proxy and kubelet into a single binary to reduce dependencies and steps required to bootstrap a cluster.

It’s a fully conformant distribution that simplifies our operations. Despite its minimal design, K3s is production ready and well suited for small to medium-sized clusters. It compacts and abstracts a lot of the overhead that comes with running a standard Kubernetes installation.

Furthermore, K3s and Kube-Hetzner come with sensible defaults. You are free and able to change advanced configurations in your cluster such as the IP ranges used for the pod and service network or change the internal cluster hostname. More on these settings later as we’ll configure them with Terraform.

K3s server configuration

Planning our cluster

For this blog we’ll deploy a standard highly-available cluster. Three control plane nodes and three worker nodes spread out across Falkenstein, Nuremberg and Helsinki.

For cost efficiency, we’ll enable the Rancher local-path provisioner to make use of the disks attached to our VPS nodes. This lets us choose between regional block storage or disks attached to our nodes using local-path, depending on the workload.

I’ll opt out of using Longhorn as the local-path provisioner combined with the block storage is sufficient for my workloads. If you require storage that is replicated across regions and independent of pod scheduling decisions, you can enable Longhorn. Do beware of performance bottlenecks when using Longhorn and benchmark it for your use case.

💡 Alternatively, you could opt for a dual-region failover setup across the two German sites. While the Helsinki region generally has good server availability and acceptable latency for production workloads, its latency is still higher than the site-to-site connection between Falkenstein and Nuremberg.

Select your desired machine type for the control plane and worker nodes. I’ll opt for CX23 control planes (2 virtual cores / 4GB ram / 40GB disk @ €3,49/mo) and CX33 (4 virtual cores / GB ram / 80GB disk @ €5,49/mo) for our workers. These instances are part of Hetzner’s shared cloud offering, meaning resources are shared with other customers on the same physical host. In my experience, I haven’t observed significant CPU steal time and do not believe that Hetzner aggressively oversubscribes their servers. That said, you should evaluate this choice carefully based on your specific workload and business requirements.

That puts us up to €26,97 a month on our six machines so far. Now we need to decide on a network configuration for our cluster.

For this post, I’ll keep it simple. We’ll opt to give each node a public IP address and whitelist our IP address to connect on port 22 (SSH). The API Server will be closed for external traffic as we’ll put a load balancer infront of our control planes.

Additionally, our traefik ingress class gets its own load balancer. This gives us a highly available, seperate entrypoint for cluster management and application traffic. These two additional load balancers (lb11) bump up the price with €5.39/mo.

A public IP address costs €1,70/mo which adds an additional €10,20 making our total €47,95/mo pre-VAT and €58,03/mo with VAT.

To me, this is a very reasonable cost for our cluster. Barring promotional discounts and free tiers, a typical managed cluster starts at $70-$75/mo on GCP GKE or AWS EKS. That is just the cost of running your cluster, not the workloads you are going to schedule on it.

However, there are some options for those looking to push down the costs further:

1. Use a single load balancer or MetalLB

Instead of using separate load balancers for the API server and ingress traffic, you could consolidate to a single load balancer that handles both. This saves €5.39/mo, bringing the total down to €52.64/mo with VAT.

Alternatively, skip Hetzner’s load balancers entirely and use MetalLB as a software load balancer running inside your cluster.

2. Remove public IP addresses and use private networking

Each public IPv4 address costs €1.70/mo, totaling €10.20/mo for six nodes. By removing public IPs and connecting to your cluster through a bastion host, NAT gateway, or VPN solution, you can significantly reduce costs and improve security posture.

Options include:

  • Bastion host: A single small node with a public IP that you SSH through to access cluster nodes
  • WireGuard VPN: Set up a WireGuard server on one node or a separate instance to provide secure private network access
  • Tailscale/Nebula: Use a mesh VPN solution for seamless private connectivity

This approach removes €10.20/mo in IP costs.

Rolling out our cluster

It’s time to actually deploy the cluster. Head over and clone our starter project to get started. Take note of the Important configurations section in the Readme file and change the values to your liking.

Now we assume that you have Terraform, Kubectl and the hcloud CLI installed. If you do not have these, install them now.

In the project directory, start by installing the terraform dependencies by running terraform init -upgrade.

Next we plan our infrastructure changes with terraform plan. Take a moment to look over the plan, it shows you exactly what terraform is going to create for you in Hetzner. If you’re satisfied with the planned changes apply them with terraform apply -auto-approve. The applying process can take up to 10 minutes.

💡 Getting stuck waiting for MicroOS to become active in the local-exec step during the apply? Make sure your machine has connectivity to the nodes and you’ve whitelisted your IP address for SSH connections.

After a successful apply we can get our kubeconfig with terraform output -raw kubeconfig > config. Move the config file to the default .kube directory ~/.kube/config for Linux & MacOS or %USERPROFILE%\.kube\config for Windows.

Now we can test connectivity with our cluster by running kubectl get nodes.

Success! My 6 node cluster has successfully rolled out with my nodes being spread over the three Hetzner zones. Checking my ~/.kube/config file I can see I am connecting to https://142.132.242.235:6443.

This should be the IP address of the controlplane load balancer, let’s verify it in the Hetzner UI.

Perfect! All resources have been created successfully. Our cluster is now up and running with:

  • 6 nodes spread across 3 availability zones
  • 2 load balancers for API server and ingress traffic
  • Private, encrypted networking between all nodes
  • Automated K3s installation and configuration
  • Ready to deploy workloads

From here, you can start deploying your applications using kubectl apply or install additional tooling like monitoring, logging, or CI/CD integrations. The cluster is production-ready and will automatically handle upgrades and maintenance through the built-in controllers we discussed earlier.

Backups

Kube-Hetzner makes use of K3s’ built-in etcd snapshot capability. This allows us to backup our cluster state to an external S3 compatible storage provider. K3s automatically creates etcd snapshots on the nodes themselves, relying solely on local snapshots leaves us vulnerable to a catastrophic node failure.

Node backups are facilitated through Hetzner’s built-in backupping mechanism.

Upgrades

KH uses Rancher’s System Upgrade Controller (SUC) to manage Kubernetes and node-level upgrades in a safe, fully automated way. SUC enables upgrades to be defined declaratively and executed natively within Kubernetes.

The controller continuously watches for Plan resources. When an upgrade is required, it selects the appropriate nodes and coordinates the rollout according to the defined strategy. This includes handling upgrade order, limiting concurrency, and applying cordon and drain rules. Each Plan clearly defines what is upgraded, how the upgrade is performed, and which nodes are affected.

Complementing the SUC, KH also deploys kured the Kubernetes Reboot Daemon. Kured handles node restarts when the OS signals that a reboot is required. Kured monitors the reboot signal and coordinates reboots across nodes managing concurrency and cordoning the nodes. You can tell kured to only reboot your nodes within set timeframes and on specified days only.

sentences.en: The project handles much of the heavy lifting involved in bootstrapping and maintaining a cluster: provisioning compute resources, configuring private networking, setting up load balancers, managing node pools, and wiring it all together.
sentences.zh: 该项目处理引导和维护集群所涉及的大部分繁重工作：配置计算资源、配置专用网络、设置负载平衡器、管理节点池以及将它们连接在一起。

sentences.en: Upgrades are automated and rolling by default, reducing manual intervention and downtime during maintenance.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，升级是自动滚动的，减少了维护期间的手动干预和停机时间。

blocks.html_en: It integrates Hetzner’s native tooling directly into your cluster, including the hcloud CSI driver and the hcloud Cloud Controller Manager (CCM). This enables Kubernetes to automatically provision and manage resources like load balancers and block storage via Kubernetes.

sentences.en: It integrates Hetzner’s native tooling directly into your cluster, including the hcloud CSI driver and the hcloud Cloud Controller Manager (CCM).
sentences.zh: 它将 Hetzner 的本机工具直接集成到您的集群中，包括 hcloud CSI 驱动程序和 hcloud 云控制器管理器 (CCM)。

sentences.en: This enables Kubernetes to automatically provision and manage resources like load balancers and block storage via Kubernetes.
sentences.zh: 这使得 Kubernetes 能够通过 Kubernetes 自动配置和管理负载均衡器和块存储等资源。

blocks.html_en: MicroOS

sentences.en: MicroOS
sentences.zh: 微操作系统

blocks.html_en: In the world of containers, less is more. The same applies for the operating system that functions as the base for our workloads. Traditional server distributions like Ubuntu, Debian or RedHat Enterprise Linux include a wide range of packages and services for general-purpose use, many of which are unnecessary for container workloads. This has led to the development of container-centric operating systems; Talos, CoreOS, Bottlerocket and flatcar just to name a few.

sentences.en: In the world of containers, less is more.
sentences.zh: 在容器的世界里，少即是多。

sentences.en: The same applies for the operating system that functions as the base for our workloads.
sentences.zh: 这同样适用于作为我们工作负载基础的操作系统。

sentences.en: Traditional server distributions like Ubuntu, Debian or RedHat Enterprise Linux include a wide range of packages and services for general-purpose use, many of which are unnecessary for container workloads.
sentences.zh: Ubuntu、Debian 或 RedHat Enterprise Linux 等传统服务器发行版包含广泛的通用软件包和服务，其中许多对于容器工作负载来说是不必要的。

sentences.en: This has led to the development of container-centric operating systems; Talos, CoreOS, Bottlerocket and flatcar just to name a few.
sentences.zh: 这导致了以容器为中心的操作系统的发展； Talos、CoreOS、Bottlerocket 和 Flatcar 仅举几例。

blocks.html_en: Kube-Hetzner utilises openSUSE’s MicroOS for all of its nodes. MicroOS is a stable operating system with a small footprint specialized in running container workloads. It sports rolling releases, atomic & transactional updates and secure updates by default. The OS is designed to be predictable, resilient and to behave the same on every boot.

sentences.en: Kube-Hetzner utilises openSUSE’s MicroOS for all of its nodes.
sentences.zh: Kube-Hetzner 在其所有节点上都使用 openSUSE 的 MicroOS。

sentences.en: MicroOS is a stable operating system with a small footprint specialized in running container workloads.
sentences.zh: MicroOS 是一个稳定的操作系统，占用空间小，专门用于运行容器工作负载。

sentences.en: It sports rolling releases, atomic & transactional updates and secure updates by default.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，它支持滚动发布、原子和事务更新以及安全更新。

sentences.en: The OS is designed to be predictable, resilient and to behave the same on every boot.
sentences.zh: 该操作系统的设计具有可预测性、弹性，并且在每次启动时都表现相同。

blocks.html_en: MicroOS is immutable and cannot be modified during runtime. Updates are applied transactionally using Btrfs snapshots, allowing the system to fully succeed or roll back to a clean state. In the event of a faulty update a previous snapshot is automatically booted with no manual interaction required.

sentences.en: MicroOS is immutable and cannot be modified during runtime.
sentences.zh: MicroOS 是不可变的，不能在运行时修改。

sentences.en: Updates are applied transactionally using Btrfs snapshots, allowing the system to fully succeed or roll back to a clean state.
sentences.zh: 使用 Btrfs 快照以事务方式应用更新，使系统能够完全成功或回滚到干净状态。

sentences.en: In the event of a faulty update a previous snapshot is automatically booted with no manual interaction required.
sentences.zh: 如果更新出现错误，将自动启动以前的快照，无需手动交互。

blocks.html_en: A read-only root filesystem also reduces our attack surface. After a container is compromised, modifying host binaries or operating system configuration is not possible. Even if an attacker gains host-level access, any changes are discarded on reboot.

sentences.en: A read-only root filesystem also reduces our attack surface.
sentences.zh: 只读根文件系统也减少了我们的攻击面。

sentences.en: After a container is compromised, modifying host binaries or operating system configuration is not possible.
sentences.zh: 容器受到威胁后，无法修改主机二进制文件或操作系统配置。

sentences.en: Even if an attacker gains host-level access, any changes are discarded on reboot.
sentences.zh: 即使攻击者获得主机级访问权限，任何更改都会在重新启动时被丢弃。

blocks.html_en: Our application workloads are managed independently from the host operating system and are not installed on the root filesystem. This prevents configuration drift and ensures all of our nodes remain interchangeable and can soundly be rolled out, replaced or recovered repeatedly.

sentences.en: Our application workloads are managed independently from the host operating system and are not installed on the root filesystem.
sentences.zh: 我们的应用程序工作负载独立于主机操作系统进行管理，并且不安装在根文件系统上。

sentences.en: This prevents configuration drift and ensures all of our nodes remain interchangeable and can soundly be rolled out, replaced or recovered repeatedly.
sentences.zh: 这可以防止配置漂移，并确保我们所有的节点保持可互换性，并且可以可靠地重复部署、替换或恢复。

blocks.html_en: K3s

The heart of our cluster consists of K3s, Rancher’s lightweight Kubernetes distribution. Designed specifically for compute-constrained environments like edge or IoT devices. K3s packages components like the Kubernetes API server, kube proxy and kubelet into a single binary to reduce dependencies and steps required to bootstrap a cluster.

sentences.en: The heart of our cluster consists of K3s, Rancher’s lightweight Kubernetes distribution.
sentences.zh: 我们集群的核心由 Rancher 的轻量级 Kubernetes 发行版 K3s 组成。

sentences.en: Designed specifically for compute-constrained environments like edge or IoT devices.
sentences.zh: 专为边缘或物联网设备等计算受限的环境而设计。

sentences.en: K3s packages components like the Kubernetes API server, kube proxy and kubelet into a single binary to reduce dependencies and steps required to bootstrap a cluster.
sentences.zh: K3s 将 Kubernetes API 服务器、kube proxy 和 kubelet 等组件打包到单个二进制文件中，以减少引导集群所需的依赖性和步骤。

blocks.html_en: It’s a fully conformant distribution that simplifies our operations. Despite its minimal design, K3s is production ready and well suited for small to medium-sized clusters. It compacts and abstracts a lot of the overhead that comes with running a standard Kubernetes installation.

sentences.en: It’s a fully conformant distribution that simplifies our operations.
sentences.zh: 这是一个完全一致的发行版，可以简化我们的操作。

sentences.en: Despite its minimal design, K3s is production ready and well suited for small to medium-sized clusters.
sentences.zh: 尽管设计简约，但 K3s 已做好生产准备，非常适合中小型集群。

sentences.en: It compacts and abstracts a lot of the overhead that comes with running a standard Kubernetes installation.
sentences.zh: 它压缩并抽象了运行标准 Kubernetes 安装所带来的大量开销。

blocks.html_en: Furthermore, K3s and Kube-Hetzner come with sensible defaults. You are free and able to change advanced configurations in your cluster such as the IP ranges used for the pod and service network or change the internal cluster hostname. More on these settings later as we’ll configure them with Terraform.

sentences.en: Furthermore, K3s and Kube-Hetzner come with sensible defaults.
sentences.zh: 此外，K3s 和 Kube-Hetzner 具有合理的默认值。

sentences.en: You are free and able to change advanced configurations in your cluster such as the IP ranges used for the pod and service network or change the internal cluster hostname.
sentences.zh: 您可以自由地更改集群中的高级配置，例如用于 pod 和服务网络的 IP 范围或更改内部集群主机名。

sentences.en: More on these settings later as we’ll configure them with Terraform.
sentences.zh: 稍后我们将使用 Terraform 配置它们，详细介绍这些设置。

blocks.html_en: K3s server configuration

sentences.en: K3s server configuration
sentences.zh: K3s服务器配置

blocks.html_en: Planning our cluster

sentences.en: Planning our cluster
sentences.zh: 规划我们的集群

blocks.html_en: For this blog we’ll deploy a standard highly-available cluster. Three control plane nodes and three worker nodes spread out across Falkenstein, Nuremberg and Helsinki.

sentences.en: For this blog we’ll deploy a standard highly-available cluster.
sentences.zh: 在本博客中，我们将部署一个标准的高可用集群。

sentences.en: Three control plane nodes and three worker nodes spread out across Falkenstein, Nuremberg and Helsinki.
sentences.zh: 三个控制平面节点和三个工作节点分布在法尔肯斯坦、纽伦堡和赫尔辛基。

blocks.html_en: For cost efficiency, we’ll enable the Rancher local-path provisioner to make use of the disks attached to our VPS nodes. This lets us choose between regional block storage or disks attached to our nodes using local-path, depending on the workload.

sentences.en: For cost efficiency, we’ll enable the Rancher local-path provisioner to make use of the disks attached to our VPS nodes.
sentences.zh: 为了提高成本效率，我们将启用 Rancher 本地路径配置程序来利用连接到我们的 VPS 节点的磁盘。

sentences.en: This lets us choose between regional block storage or disks attached to our nodes using local-path, depending on the workload.
sentences.zh: 这让我们可以根据工作负载，在区域块存储或使用本地路径附加到节点的磁盘之间进行选择。

blocks.html_en: I’ll opt out of using Longhorn as the local-path provisioner combined with the block storage is sufficient for my workloads. If you require storage that is replicated across regions and independent of pod scheduling decisions, you can enable Longhorn. Do beware of performance bottlenecks when using Longhorn and benchmark it for your use case.

sentences.en: I’ll opt out of using Longhorn as the local-path provisioner combined with the block storage is sufficient for my workloads.
sentences.zh: 我将选择不使用 Longhorn，因为本地路径配置程序与块存储相结合足以满足我的工作负载。

sentences.en: If you require storage that is replicated across regions and independent of pod scheduling decisions, you can enable Longhorn.
sentences.zh: 如果您需要跨区域复制存储并且独立于 Pod 调度决策，则可以启用 Longhorn。

sentences.en: Do beware of performance bottlenecks when using Longhorn and benchmark it for your use case.
sentences.zh: 使用 Longhorn 时请务必注意性能瓶颈，并针对您的用例对其进行基准测试。

blocks.html_en: 💡 Alternatively, you could opt for a dual-region failover setup across the two German sites. While the Helsinki region generally has good server availability and acceptable latency for production workloads, its latency is still higher than the site-to-site connection between Falkenstein and Nuremberg.

sentences.en: 💡 Alternatively, you could opt for a dual-region failover setup across the two German sites.
sentences.zh: 💡 或者，您可以选择跨两个德国站点的双区域故障转移设置。

sentences.en: While the Helsinki region generally has good server availability and acceptable latency for production workloads, its latency is still higher than the site-to-site connection between Falkenstein and Nuremberg.
sentences.zh: 虽然赫尔辛基地区通常具有良好的服务器可用性和可接受的生产工作负载延迟，但其延迟仍然高于 Falkenstein 和纽伦堡之间的站点到站点连接。

blocks.html_en: 💡 Alternatively, you could opt for a dual-region failover setup across the two German sites. While the Helsinki region generally has good server availability and acceptable latency for production workloads, its latency is still higher than the site-to-site connection between Falkenstein and Nuremberg.

sentences.en: 💡 Alternatively, you could opt for a dual-region failover setup across the two German sites.
sentences.zh: 💡 或者，您可以选择跨两个德国站点的双区域故障转移设置。

sentences.en: While the Helsinki region generally has good server availability and acceptable latency for production workloads, its latency is still higher than the site-to-site connection between Falkenstein and Nuremberg.
sentences.zh: 虽然赫尔辛基地区通常具有良好的服务器可用性和可接受的生产工作负载延迟，但其延迟仍然高于 Falkenstein 和纽伦堡之间的站点到站点连接。

blocks.html_en: Select your desired machine type for the control plane and worker nodes. I’ll opt for CX23 control planes (2 virtual cores / 4GB ram / 40GB disk @ €3,49/mo) and CX33 (4 virtual cores / GB ram / 80GB disk @ €5,49/mo) for our workers. These instances are part of Hetzner’s shared cloud offering, meaning resources are shared with other customers on the same physical host. In my experience, I haven’t observed significant CPU steal time and do not believe that Hetzner aggressively oversubscribes their servers. That said, you should evaluate this choice carefully based on your specific workload and business requirements.

sentences.en: Select your desired machine type for the control plane and worker nodes.
sentences.zh: 为控制平面和工作节点选择所需的机器类型。

sentences.en: I’ll opt for CX23 control planes (2 virtual cores / 4GB ram / 40GB disk @ €3,49/mo) and CX33 (4 virtual cores / GB ram / 80GB disk @ €5,49/mo) for our workers.
sentences.zh: 我将为我们的员工选择 CX23 控制平面（2 个虚拟核心/4GB 内存/40GB 磁盘 @ €3,49/月）和 CX33（4 个虚拟核心/GB 内存/80GB 磁盘 @ €5,49/月）。

sentences.en: These instances are part of Hetzner’s shared cloud offering, meaning resources are shared with other customers on the same physical host.
sentences.zh: 这些实例是 Hetzner 共享云产品的一部分，这意味着资源可以与同一物理主机上的其他客户共享。

sentences.en: In my experience, I haven’t observed significant CPU steal time and do not believe that Hetzner aggressively oversubscribes their servers.
sentences.zh: 根据我的经验，我没有观察到明显的 CPU 窃取时间，并且不相信 Hetzner 积极超额订阅他们的服务器。

sentences.en: That said, you should evaluate this choice carefully based on your specific workload and business requirements.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，您应该根据您的特定工作负载和业务要求仔细评估此选择。

blocks.html_en: That puts us up to €26,97 a month on our six machines so far. Now we need to decide on a network configuration for our cluster.

sentences.en: That puts us up to €26,97 a month on our six machines so far.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，我们的六台机器每月的费用为 26.97 欧元。

sentences.en: Now we need to decide on a network configuration for our cluster.
sentences.zh: 现在我们需要决定集群的网络配置。

blocks.html_en: For this post, I’ll keep it simple. We’ll opt to give each node a public IP address and whitelist our IP address to connect on port 22 (SSH). The API Server will be closed for external traffic as we’ll put a load balancer infront of our control planes.

sentences.en: For this post, I’ll keep it simple.
sentences.zh: 对于这篇文章，我会保持简单。

sentences.en: We’ll opt to give each node a public IP address and whitelist our IP address to connect on port 22 (SSH).
sentences.zh: 我们将选择为每个节点提供一个公共 IP 地址，并将我们的 IP 地址列入白名单以在端口 22 (SSH) 上进行连接。

sentences.en: The API Server will be closed for external traffic as we’ll put a load balancer infront of our control planes.
sentences.zh: API 服务器将对外部流量关闭，因为我们将在控制平面前面放置一个负载均衡器。

blocks.html_en: Additionally, our traefik ingress class gets its own load balancer. This gives us a highly available, seperate entrypoint for cluster management and application traffic. These two additional load balancers (lb11) bump up the price with €5.39/mo.

sentences.en: Additionally, our traefik ingress class gets its own load balancer.
sentences.zh: 此外，我们的 traefik 入口类有自己的负载均衡器。

sentences.en: This gives us a highly available, seperate entrypoint for cluster management and application traffic.
sentences.zh: 这为我们提供了一个高度可用、独立的集群管理和应用程序流量入口点。

sentences.en: These two additional load balancers (lb11) bump up the price with €5.39/mo.
sentences.zh: 这两个额外的负载均衡器 (lb11) 将价格提高到 5.39 欧元/月。

blocks.html_en: A public IP address costs €1,70/mo which adds an additional €10,20 making our total €47,95/mo pre-VAT and €58,03/mo with VAT.

sentences.en: A public IP address costs €1,70/mo which adds an additional €10,20 making our total €47,95/mo pre-VAT and €58,03/mo with VAT.
sentences.zh: 公共 IP 地址的费用为 1,70 欧元/月，另外增加了 10,20 欧元，使我们的增值税前总额为 47,95 欧元/月，含增值税为 58,03 欧元/月。

blocks.html_en: To me, this is a very reasonable cost for our cluster. Barring promotional discounts and free tiers, a typical managed cluster starts at $70-$75/mo on GCP GKE or AWS EKS. That is just the cost of running your cluster, not the workloads you are going to schedule on it.

sentences.en: To me, this is a very reasonable cost for our cluster.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，这对于我们的集群来说是非常合理的成本。

sentences.en: Barring promotional discounts and free tiers, a typical managed cluster starts at $70-$75/mo on GCP GKE or AWS EKS.
sentences.zh: 排除促销折扣和免费套餐，GCP GKE 或 AWS EKS 上的典型托管集群起价为 70-75 美元/月。

sentences.en: That is just the cost of running your cluster, not the workloads you are going to schedule on it.
sentences.zh: 这只是运行集群的成本，而不是您要在其上安排的工作负载。

blocks.html_en: However, there are some options for those looking to push down the costs further:

sentences.en: However, there are some options for those looking to push down the costs further:
sentences.zh: 然而，对于那些希望进一步降低成本的人来说，有一些选择：

blocks.html_en: 1. Use a single load balancer or MetalLB

sentences.en: Use a single load balancer or MetalLB
sentences.zh: 使用单个负载均衡器或 MetalLB

blocks.html_en: Instead of using separate load balancers for the API server and ingress traffic, you could consolidate to a single load balancer that handles both. This saves €5.39/mo, bringing the total down to €52.64/mo with VAT.

sentences.en: Instead of using separate load balancers for the API server and ingress traffic, you could consolidate to a single load balancer that handles both.
sentences.zh: 您可以将 API 服务器和入口流量合并到一个负载均衡器来处理这两者，而不是使用单独的负载均衡器。

sentences.en: This saves €5.39/mo, bringing the total down to €52.64/mo with VAT.
sentences.zh: 这样可节省 5.39 欧元/月，含增值税总额降至 52.64 欧元/月。

blocks.html_en: Alternatively, skip Hetzner’s load balancers entirely and use MetalLB as a software load balancer running inside your cluster.

sentences.en: Alternatively, skip Hetzner’s load balancers entirely and use MetalLB as a software load balancer running inside your cluster.
sentences.zh: 或者，完全跳过 Hetzner 的负载均衡器，并使用 MetalLB 作为在集群内运行的软件负载均衡器。

blocks.html_en: 2. Remove public IP addresses and use private networking

sentences.en: Remove public IP addresses and use private networking
sentences.zh: 删除公共 IP 地址并使用专用网络

blocks.html_en: Each public IPv4 address costs €1.70/mo, totaling €10.20/mo for six nodes. By removing public IPs and connecting to your cluster through a bastion host, NAT gateway, or VPN solution, you can significantly reduce costs and improve security posture.

sentences.en: Each public IPv4 address costs €1.70/mo, totaling €10.20/mo for six nodes.
sentences.zh: 每个公共 IPv4 地址的费用为 1.70 欧元/月，六个节点总计 10.20 欧元/月。

sentences.en: By removing public IPs and connecting to your cluster through a bastion host, NAT gateway, or VPN solution, you can significantly reduce costs and improve security posture.
sentences.zh: 通过删除公共 IP 并通过堡垒主机、NAT 网关或 VPN 解决方案连接到集群，您可以显着降低成本并改善安全状况。

blocks.html_en: Options include:

sentences.en: Options include:
sentences.zh: 选项包括：

blocks.html_en: • Bastion host: A single small node with a public IP that you SSH through to access cluster nodes

sentences.en: Bastion host: A single small node with a public IP that you SSH through to access cluster nodes
sentences.zh: 堡垒主机：具有公共 IP 的单个小节点，您可以通过 SSH 访问集群节点

blocks.html_en: • WireGuard VPN: Set up a WireGuard server on one node or a separate instance to provide secure private network access

sentences.en: WireGuard VPN: Set up a WireGuard server on one node or a separate instance to provide secure private network access
sentences.zh: WireGuard VPN：在一个节点或单独的实例上设置 WireGuard 服务器以提供安全的专用网络访问

blocks.html_en: • Tailscale/Nebula: Use a mesh VPN solution for seamless private connectivity

sentences.en: Tailscale/Nebula: Use a mesh VPN solution for seamless private connectivity
sentences.zh: Tailscale/Nebula：使用网状 VPN 解决方案实现无缝私人连接

blocks.html_en: This approach removes €10.20/mo in IP costs.

sentences.en: This approach removes €10.20/mo in IP costs.
sentences.zh: 这种方法消除了 10.20 欧元/月的 IP 成本。

blocks.html_en: Rolling out our cluster

sentences.en: Rolling out our cluster
sentences.zh: 推出我们的集群

blocks.html_en: It’s time to actually deploy the cluster. Head over and clone our starter project to get started. Take note of the Important configurations section in the Readme file and change the values to your liking.

sentences.en: It’s time to actually deploy the cluster.
sentences.zh: 是时候实际部署集群了。

sentences.en: Head over and clone our starter project to get started.
sentences.zh: 直接克隆我们的入门项目即可开始。

sentences.en: Take note of the Important configurations section in the Readme file and change the values to your liking.
sentences.zh: 记下自述文件中的重要配置部分，并根据您的喜好更改值。

blocks.html_en: Now we assume that you have Terraform, Kubectl and the hcloud CLI installed. If you do not have these, install them now.

sentences.en: Now we assume that you have Terraform, Kubectl and the hcloud CLI installed.
sentences.zh: 现在我们假设您已经安装了 Terraform、Kubectl 和 hcloud CLI。

sentences.en: If you do not have these, install them now.
sentences.zh: 如果您没有这些，请立即安装。

blocks.html_en: In the project directory, start by installing the terraform dependencies by running terraform init -upgrade.

sentences.en: In the project directory, start by installing the terraform dependencies by running terraform init -upgrade.
sentences.zh: 在项目目录中，首先通过运行 terraform init -upgrade 安装 terraform 依赖项。

blocks.html_en: Next we plan our infrastructure changes with terraform plan. Take a moment to look over the plan, it shows you exactly what terraform is going to create for you in Hetzner. If you’re satisfied with the planned changes apply them with terraform apply -auto-approve. The applying process can take up to 10 minutes.

sentences.en: Next we plan our infrastructure changes with terraform plan.
sentences.zh: 接下来，我们使用 Terraform 计划来规划基础设施变更。

sentences.en: Take a moment to look over the plan, it shows you exactly what terraform is going to create for you in Hetzner.
sentences.zh: 花点时间看一下该计划，它准确地向您展示了 terraform 将在 Hetzner 为您创造什么。

blocks.html_en: Backups

sentences.en: Backups
sentences.zh: 备份

blocks.html_en: Kube-Hetzner makes use of K3s’ built-in etcd snapshot capability. This allows us to backup our cluster state to an external S3 compatible storage provider. K3s automatically creates etcd snapshots on the nodes themselves, relying solely on local snapshots leaves us vulnerable to a catastrophic node failure.

sentences.en: Kube-Hetzner makes use of K3s’ built-in etcd snapshot capability.
sentences.zh: Kube-Hetzner 利用 K3s 的内置 etcd 快照功能。

sentences.en: This allows us to backup our cluster state to an external S3 compatible storage provider.
sentences.zh: 这使我们能够将集群状态备份到外部 S3 兼容存储提供商。

sentences.en: K3s automatically creates etcd snapshots on the nodes themselves, relying solely on local snapshots leaves us vulnerable to a catastrophic node failure.
sentences.zh: K3s 会自动在节点本身上创建 etcd 快照，仅依赖本地快照会让我们容易遭受灾难性的节点故障。

blocks.html_en: Node backups are facilitated through Hetzner’s built-in backupping mechanism.

sentences.en: Node backups are facilitated through Hetzner’s built-in backupping mechanism.
sentences.zh: 通过 Hetzner 的内置备份机制可以促进节点备份。

blocks.html_en: Upgrades

sentences.en: Upgrades
sentences.zh: 升级

blocks.html_en: KH uses Rancher’s System Upgrade Controller (SUC) to manage Kubernetes and node-level upgrades in a safe, fully automated way. SUC enables upgrades to be defined declaratively and executed natively within Kubernetes.

sentences.en: KH uses Rancher’s System Upgrade Controller (SUC) to manage Kubernetes and node-level upgrades in a safe, fully automated way.
sentences.zh: KH 使用 Rancher 的系统升级控制器（SUC）以安全、全自动的方式管理 Kubernetes 和节点级升级。

sentences.en: SUC enables upgrades to be defined declaratively and executed natively within Kubernetes.
sentences.zh: SUC 允许以声明方式定义升级并在 Kubernetes 内本机执行。

blocks.html_en: The controller continuously watches for Plan resources. When an upgrade is required, it selects the appropriate nodes and coordinates the rollout according to the defined strategy. This includes handling upgrade order, limiting concurrency, and applying cordon and drain rules. Each Plan clearly defines what is upgraded, how the upgrade is performed, and which nodes are affected.

sentences.en: The controller continuously watches for Plan resources.
sentences.zh: 控制器持续监视计划资源。

sentences.en: When an upgrade is required, it selects the appropriate nodes and coordinates the rollout according to the defined strategy.
sentences.zh: 当需要升级时，它会选择适当的节点并根据定义的策略协调部署。

sentences.en: This includes handling upgrade order, limiting concurrency, and applying cordon and drain rules.
sentences.zh: 这包括处理升级订单、限制并发以及应用警戒线和耗尽规则。

sentences.en: Each Plan clearly defines what is upgraded, how the upgrade is performed, and which nodes are affected.
sentences.zh: 每个计划都明确定义了升级内容、升级方式以及受影响的节点。

blocks.html_en: Complementing the SUC, KH also deploys kured the Kubernetes Reboot Daemon. Kured handles node restarts when the OS signals that a reboot is required. Kured monitors the reboot signal and coordinates reboots across nodes managing concurrency and cordoning the nodes. You can tell kured to only reboot your nodes within set timeframes and on specified days only.

sentences.en: Complementing the SUC, KH also deploys kured the Kubernetes Reboot Daemon.
sentences.zh: 作为 SUC 的补充，KH 还部署了 kured Kubernetes Reboot Daemon。

sentences.en: Kured handles node restarts when the OS signals that a reboot is required.
sentences.zh: 当操作系统发出需要重新启动的信号时，Kured 会处理节点重新启动。

sentences.en: Kured monitors the reboot signal and coordinates reboots across nodes managing concurrency and cordoning the nodes.
sentences.zh: Kured 监视重新启动信号并协调跨节点的重新启动，管理并发性并封锁节点。

sentences.en: You can tell kured to only reboot your nodes within set timeframes and on specified days only.
sentences.zh: 您可以告诉 kured 仅在设定的时间范围内和指定的日期重新启动节点。

content_html_zh:
该博客的入门项目可以在 github.com/qstarsit/hcloud-kube-hetzner 找到

Kubernetes 是一个出色的工作负载 API，社区构建了一个高度可扩展的标准，该标准从我们的应用程序部署中抽象出底层机器和基础设施。 然而，云原生生态系统可能会带来供应商锁定感。 托管负载平衡、无缝节点自动扩展、广泛的存储选项、集成网络和完善的操作工具等功能是您需要支付溢价的。

自己运行和操作 Kubernetes，“艰难的方式”，可能会让人不知所措，而且非常耗时。 这就是为什么在这篇文章中，我们将研究 terraform-hcloud-kube-hetzner。 一个开源 Terraform 项目，可简化在 Hetzner Cloud 上部署生产就绪的 K3s 集群，在易用性、可靠性和成本效率之间取得平衡。 同时保持我们的欧洲血统🇪🇺。

赫茨纳

Hetzner 是一家德国基础设施提供商，以合理且可预测的价格提供高性能硬件而享有盛誉。 与超大规模提供商相比，它们的功能较少，专注于把很少的事情做好。 这使他们成为独立黑客和初创公司中受欢迎的托管提供商。

近年来，他们的云产品已经成熟很多，并具有可靠的 API 来管理资源。 它具有专用网络、负载平衡器、网络附加存储和虚拟机。 这使得它成为在更具成本效益的平台上寻求适当自动化的团队的可靠替代方案。

Hetzner 主要在欧洲运营现代数据中心，同时还在美国和亚洲提供数据中心。 鉴于最近对主权数据中心的关注，Hetzner 是一家德国公司这一事实增加了人们对数据主权的信心。

这是赫兹纳

Kube-Hetzner 是一个优化的、易于使用的 terraform 模块，用于设置（高度可用）自动升级的集群。 它整理了在 Hetzner Cloud 上可靠运行 K3 所需的许多最佳实践。

该项目处理引导和维护集群所涉及的大部分繁重工作：配置计算资源、配置专用网络、设置负载平衡器、管理节点池以及将它们连接在一起。 默认情况下，升级是自动滚动的，减少了维护期间的手动干预和停机时间。

它将 Hetzner 的本机工具直接集成到您的集群中，包括 hcloud CSI 驱动程序和 hcloud 云控制器管理器 (CCM)。 这使得 Kubernetes 能够通过 Kubernetes 自动配置和管理负载均衡器和块存储等资源。

微操作系统

在容器的世界里，少即是多。 这同样适用于作为我们工作负载基础的操作系统。 Ubuntu、Debian 或 RedHat Enterprise Linux 等传统服务器发行版包含广泛的通用软件包和服务，其中许多对于容器工作负载来说是不必要的。 这导致了以容器为中心的操作系统的发展； Talos、CoreOS、Bottlerocket 和 Flatcar 仅举几例。

Kube-Hetzner 在其所有节点上都使用 openSUSE 的 MicroOS。 MicroOS 是一个稳定的操作系统，占用空间小，专门用于运行容器工作负载。 默认情况下，它支持滚动发布、原子和事务更新以及安全更新。 该操作系统的设计具有可预测性、弹性，并且在每次启动时都表现相同。

MicroOS 是不可变的，不能在运行时修改。 使用 Btrfs 快照以事务方式应用更新，使系统能够完全成功或回滚到干净状态。 如果更新出现错误，将自动启动以前的快照，无需手动交互。

只读根文件系统也减少了我们的攻击面。 容器受到威胁后，无法修改主机二进制文件或操作系统配置。 即使攻击者获得主机级访问权限，任何更改都会在重新启动时被丢弃。

我们的应用程序工作负载独立于主机操作系统进行管理，并且不安装在根文件系统上。 这可以防止配置漂移，并确保我们所有的节点保持可互换性，并且可以可靠地重复部署、替换或恢复。

K3s

我们集群的核心由 Rancher 的轻量级 Kubernetes 发行版 K3s 组成。 专为边缘或物联网设备等计算受限的环境而设计。 K3s 将 Kubernetes API 服务器、kube proxy 和 kubelet 等组件打包到单个二进制文件中，以减少引导集群所需的依赖性和步骤。

这是一个完全一致的发行版，可以简化我们的操作。 尽管设计简约，但 K3s 已做好生产准备，非常适合中小型集群。 它压缩并抽象了运行标准 Kubernetes 安装所带来的大量开销。

此外，K3s 和 Kube-Hetzner 具有合理的默认值。 您可以自由地更改集群中的高级配置，例如用于 pod 和服务网络的 IP 范围或更改内部集群主机名。 稍后我们将使用 Terraform 配置它们，详细介绍这些设置。

K3s服务器配置

规划我们的集群

在本博客中，我们将部署一个标准的高可用集群。 三个控制平面节点和三个工作节点分布在法尔肯斯坦、纽伦堡和赫尔辛基。

为了提高成本效率，我们将启用 Rancher 本地路径配置程序来利用连接到我们的 VPS 节点的磁盘。 这让我们可以根据工作负载，在区域块存储或使用本地路径附加到节点的磁盘之间进行选择。

我将选择不使用 Longhorn，因为本地路径配置程序与块存储相结合足以满足我的工作负载。 如果您需要跨区域复制存储并且独立于 Pod 调度决策，则可以启用 Longhorn。 使用 Longhorn 时请务必注意性能瓶颈，并针对您的用例对其进行基准测试。

💡 或者，您可以选择跨两个德国站点的双区域故障转移设置。 虽然赫尔辛基地区通常具有良好的服务器可用性和可接受的生产工作负载延迟，但其延迟仍然高于 Falkenstein 和纽伦堡之间的站点到站点连接。

为控制平面和工作节点选择所需的机器类型。 我将为我们的员工选择 CX23 控制平面（2 个虚拟核心/4GB 内存/40GB 磁盘 @ €3,49/月）和 CX33（4 个虚拟核心/GB 内存/80GB 磁盘 @ €5,49/月）。 这些实例是 Hetzner 共享云产品的一部分，这意味着资源可以与同一物理主机上的其他客户共享。 根据我的经验，我没有观察到明显的 CPU 窃取时间，并且不相信 Hetzner 积极超额订阅他们的服务器。 也就是说，您应该根据您的特定工作负载和业务要求仔细评估此选择。

到目前为止，我们的六台机器每月的费用为 26.97 欧元。 现在我们需要决定集群的网络配置。

对于这篇文章，我会保持简单。 我们将选择为每个节点提供一个公共 IP 地址，并将我们的 IP 地址列入白名单以在端口 22 (SSH) 上进行连接。 API 服务器将对外部流量关闭，因为我们将在控制平面前面放置一个负载均衡器。

此外，我们的 traefik 入口类有自己的负载均衡器。 这为我们提供了一个高度可用、独立的集群管理和应用程序流量入口点。 这两个额外的负载均衡器 (lb11) 将价格提高到 5.39 欧元/月。

公共 IP 地址的费用为 1,70 欧元/月，另外增加了 10,20 欧元，使我们的增值税前总额为 47,95 欧元/月，含增值税为 58,03 欧元/月。

对我来说，这对于我们的集群来说是非常合理的成本。 排除促销折扣和免费套餐，GCP GKE 或 AWS EKS 上的典型托管集群起价为 70-75 美元/月。 这只是运行集群的成本，而不是您要在其上安排的工作负载。

然而，对于那些希望进一步降低成本的人来说，有一些选择：

1. 使用单个负载均衡器或 MetalLB

您可以将 API 服务器和入口流量合并到一个负载均衡器来处理这两者，而不是使用单独的负载均衡器。 这样可节省 5.39 欧元/月，含增值税总额降至 52.64 欧元/月。

或者，完全跳过 Hetzner 的负载均衡器，并使用 MetalLB 作为在集群内运行的软件负载均衡器。

2. 删除公共 IP 地址并使用专用网络

每个公共 IPv4 地址的费用为 1.70 欧元/月，六个节点总计 10.20 欧元/月。 通过删除公共 IP 并通过堡垒主机、NAT 网关或 VPN 解决方案连接到集群，您可以显着降低成本并改善安全状况。

选项包括：

  • 堡垒主机：具有公共 IP 的单个小节点，您可以通过 SSH 访问集群节点
  • WireGuard VPN：在一个节点或单独的实例上设置 WireGuard 服务器以提供安全的专用网络访问
  • Tailscale/Nebula：使用网状 VPN 解决方案实现无缝私人连接

这种方法消除了 10.20 欧元/月的 IP 成本。

推出我们的集群

是时候实际部署集群了。 直接克隆我们的入门项目即可开始。 记下自述文件中的重要配置部分，并根据您的喜好更改值。

现在我们假设您已经安装了 Terraform、Kubectl 和 hcloud CLI。 如果您没有这些，请立即安装。

在项目目录中，首先通过运行 terraform init -upgrade 安装 terraform 依赖项。

接下来，我们使用 Terraform 计划来规划基础设施变更。 花点时间看一下该计划，它准确地向您展示了 terraform 将在 Hetzner 为您创造什么。 如果您对计划的更改感到满意，请使用 terraform apply -auto-approve 应用它们。 申请过程最多可能需要 10 分钟。

💡 在应用期间等待 MicroOS 在本地执行步骤中激活？ 确保您的计算机已连接到节点，并且您已将 SSH 连接的 IP 地址列入白名单。

成功应用后，我们可以使用 terraform 输出 -raw kubeconfig > config 获取 kubeconfig。 将配置文件移至默认 .kube 目录 ~/.kube/config（对于 Linux 和 MacOS）或 %USERPROFILE%\.kube\config（对于 Windows）。

现在我们可以通过运行 kubectl get Nodes 来测试与集群的连接。

成功！ 我的 6 节点集群已成功推出，我的节点分布在三个 Hetzner 区域。 检查我的 ~/.kube/config 文件，我可以看到我正在连接到 https://142.132.242.235:6443。

这应该是控制平面负载均衡器的 IP 地址，让我们在 Hetzner UI 中验证它。

完美的！ 所有资源已成功创建。 我们的集群现已启动并运行：

  • 6 个节点分布在 3 个可用区
  • 2 个用于 API 服务器和入口流量的负载均衡器
  • 所有节点之间的私有加密网络
  • 自动化 K3s 安装和配置
  • 准备部署工作负载

从这里，您可以开始使用 kubectl apply 部署应用程序或安装其他工具，例如监控、日志记录或 CI/CD 集成。 该集群已做好生产准备，并将通过我们之前讨论的内置控制器自动处理升级和维护。

备份

Kube-Hetzner 利用 K3s 的内置 etcd 快照功能。 这使我们能够将集群状态备份到外部 S3 兼容存储提供商。 K3s 会自动在节点本身上创建 etcd 快照，仅依赖本地快照会让我们容易遭受灾难性的节点故障。

通过 Hetzner 的内置备份机制可以促进节点备份。

升级

KH 使用 Rancher 的系统升级控制器（SUC）以安全、全自动的方式管理 Kubernetes 和节点级升级。 SUC 允许以声明方式定义升级并在 Kubernetes 内本机执行。

控制器持续监视计划资源。 当需要升级时，它会选择适当的节点并根据定义的策略协调部署。 这包括处理升级订单、限制并发以及应用警戒线和耗尽规则。 每个计划都明确定义了升级内容、升级方式以及受影响的节点。

作为 SUC 的补充，KH 还部署了 kured Kubernetes Reboot Daemon。 当操作系统发出需要重新启动的信号时，Kured 会处理节点重新启动。 Kured 监视重新启动信号并协调跨节点的重新启动，管理并发性并封锁节点。 您可以告诉 kured 仅在设定的时间范围内和指定的日期重新启动节点。
